page: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz with no hyperthreading enabled. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance runs on the KVM hypervisor and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also notable, with the Passmark memory score and large-block read-only memory bandwidth (256MB) ranking in the top tier. Multi-threaded decompression tasks via bzip3 show strong performance, while multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall within the average range. The server demonstrates average throughput for database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving.

The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention, though the lack of hyperthreading limits concurrent thread execution compared to hyperthreaded alternatives. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a compute-heavy design, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring dedicated CPU cycles over massive memory footprints. This profile is suitable for CPU-bound tasks such as software compilation, text processing, medium-scale web serving, and lightweight LLM inference text generation.
description: The OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It delivers 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs at 2.3 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core), a 400 GB local NVMe SSD, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU and memory read performance, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. The dedicated core allocation provides predictable compute performance, making this instance suitable for CPU-bound workloads, software compilation, text processing, and medium-scale web serving where dedicated processing power is prioritized over high memory density.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory read benchmark performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-32 is a compute-optimized server with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
32 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory read benchmark results
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and CPU-bound applications
